dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.2 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Fixed the MCP connector toggle in Settings → Local MCP — turning a connector OFF now actually disables its tools. Verified end-to-end across all five connectors (Slack, ClickUp, Google, GitHub, GitLab): OFF drops the service's tools from tools/list immediately on the next message; ON restores them. Shared connector toggle is now authoritative. A stale per-account Enabled flag left over from an older iCloud restore can no longer keep a service in tools/list after the shared toggle is flipped off. Registry auto-prunes stale per-account Enabled flags at load time so debug dumps and cloud bundle pushes reflect the same state the UI shows.

Fixed: toggling MCP connectors silently no-ops (issue #2). Root cause was the Swift CLI client sending bool flags as `--enabled false` (space-separated), which Go's flag parser treats as `--enabled=true` with `false` dropped as an ignored positional. Every OFF toggle therefore persisted the opposite value. Both connector and per-tool toggles fixed.
